# Klef

> Klef (klef.sh) is a personal, zero-knowledge tool for syncing .env files
> across machines. Environment files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM in the
> browser before upload; the server stores only ciphertext it cannot decrypt.
> Free, open source (AGPL-3.0), and self-hostable.

Key facts:

- Zero-knowledge / end-to-end encrypted: the server never sees file contents,
  the master passphrase, the recovery key, or any decryption-capable key.
- Envelope encryption: a random per-account data encryption key (DEK) encrypts
  files; a key encryption key derived from the passphrase via Argon2id
  (19 MiB, t=2; PBKDF2-SHA-256 600k fallback) wraps the DEK.
- A 128-bit recovery key (KLEF-XXXXX-...) is shown exactly once at setup. If
  both the passphrase and the recovery key are lost, data is unrecoverable by
  design.
- Sign-in (Google OAuth or passkeys) is separate from unlocking, which happens
  entirely in the browser. Auto-lock and manual lock clear keys from memory.
- Every save creates a new version, so files have history and restore.
- No analytics, trackers, ads, or third-party scripts.
- Runs on Cloudflare Workers with D1; self-hostable on your own account.
- Built by Beny Dishon K (https://beny.one).

## Pages

- [Home](https://klef.sh/): what Klef is and how it works
- [Security](https://klef.sh/security): the zero-knowledge encryption design in detail
- [Terms](https://klef.sh/terms): terms of service
- [Privacy](https://klef.sh/privacy): what is stored and what never is

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/BenyD/klef): full source code, AGPL-3.0
